title: Antan Azzameen

{{Quote|Family means more to me than the Empire or the Rebellion.|Antan Azzameen|<ref name="XWA" />}}
'''Antan Azzameen''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who was the brother of [[Tomaas Azzameen/Legends|Tomaas Azzameen]] and co-founder of the shipping business [[Twin Suns Transport Services/Legends|Twin Suns Transport Services]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. He disapproved his brother's, niece's and nephews' involvement with the Rebellion, and decided to join the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].<ref name="XWA" />

==Biography==
Earlier in his life, Antan was rumored to have dueled a [[Prince/Legends|prince]] from [[Jar'Kai (city)|Jar'Kai]] and won, claiming a [[Jar'Kai dueling saber|pair of swords]], which became very precious to him. He never fathered any children, in contrast to his brother, who had four. He had always said that [[Ace Azzameen/Legends|Ace]] was a good boy.

When Tomaas and Galin in the ''[[Otana/Legends|Otana]]'' arrived at [[Twin Suns Station (space station)|Twin Sun Station]] for repairs after a run-in with Imperials at Hoth, Antan refused, knowing that they could reach [[Azzameen Station/Legends|home]] themselves. A lot of customers had been waiting in line for repairs, and Antan was not willing to give priority to [[Azzameen family|his own family]], especially since they weren't sure if the Imperials had identified the ''Otana'' or not.

When Antan learned about Ace's discovery of [[Spice/Legends|spice]] in a cargo storage area left behind by the ''[[Enkidu]]'', a [[Viraxo]] ship, he formulated a plan to make the Viraxo's plan backfire by delivering it to [[Viraxo 54]], then provoking an Imperial response to investigate and discover the spice. Things didn't go as [[Raid on Viraxo 54|planned]], and the ''[[Selu]]'' was lost. After Tomaas and Galin were lost when delivering [[Bacta/Legends|bacta]] to the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebels]], he, along with [[Emon Azzameen]], went to gather what they could from their storage facilities. However, while doing so he got captured by the Viraxo, although Emon managed to escape. After Ace joined the Rebellion, Emon learned that Antan was being held on an [[XQ5 Platform]] called [[Viraxo Industries (platform)|Viraxo Industries]]. Emon and Ace [[Rescue of Antan Azzameen|rescued]] Antan from the platform, and Antan gave Ace his prized [[Jar'Kai dueling saber]]s as a reward. He was also part of the plot to [[Assassination attempt on K'Armyn Viraxo|kill]] [[K'Armyn Viraxo]] in the ''[[Highroller]]'' but accidentally placed Ace and Emon into a trap, which the two managed to escape.

Antan then [[e-mail]]ed Ace and asked to inform him about the Rebellion's movements in order to "avoid any Rebel and Imperial encounters in his travels." This request made the siblings suspicious. Suspicions began to flare when the Rebels [[Battle of Azzameen Station|attacked]] the Azzameen home base, which had been sold to a mercenary group that was harassing the [[Bothan/Legends|Bothans]]. Antan's ship, the ''[[Big Score]]'', was seen operating in the area by the Azzameens on a mission to retake the station.

The whereabouts of Antan Azzameen during the intervening time was unclear, but Antan contacted Ace and [[Aeron Azzameen|Aeron]] and promised to explain to them why he was there. He also asked them to help him rescue their captured family members [[Tomaas Azzameen/Legends|Tomaas]] and [[Galin Azzameen/Legends|Galin]], claiming that it was he who was the leader of the mercenary group trying to split the Alliance apart in exchange for the lives of Tomaas and Galin. After ''[[Andrasta]]'' and ''Otana'' followed ''Big Score'' to a [[Golan III Space Defense NovaGun|Golan III platform]] near [[Kessel/Legends|Kessel]] called [[Kessel Station]], Antan [[Mission to Kessel Station|betrayed]] Ace, Aeron, and Emon to the Empire, apparently in exchange for a pardon from whatever crimes he had been charged with, showing a complete lack of family allegiance. The Azzameen siblings were able to defend themselves against the incoming [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighters]] until family friend [[Dunari]] unexpectedly showed up and helped them escape the ambush.<ref name="XWA" />

==Personality and traits==
[[File:TwinSunVid2.jpg|thumb|left|Antan Azzameen inside [[Twin Suns Station (space station)|Twin Sun Station's]] command center]]
Unlike all of the other members of the Azzameen family, Antan had little, if any, Rebel sympathies. He was very uncomfortable with his family doing business with the Rebel Alliance or becoming members of the Alliance. He was willing to refuse help to his family members or disagree with their ideas to support the Rebels. Antan did, however, hold a grudge against the Viraxo.<ref name="XWA Characters" /> He viewed Tomaas's efforts to work out a deal with them with disdain, and it was only when a Rebel mission involved stealing warheads from the Viraxo due to be delivered to the Empire that he agreed to lend aid to the Rebels.<ref name="XWA" />

Antan was able to make shrewd plans, but they occasionally went wrong, such as the attack on Viraxo 54, which involved losing the ''Selu'', and the attack on the ''Highroller'' when somehow K'Armyn Viraxo had gotten wind of Antan's plan and attempted to counter it, which placed Ace and Emon in jeopardy.

He was not above making deals with the Empire to save his own skin, even at the risk of the last remnants of his family.<ref name="XWA" />

==Appearances==
